reviews.author: RenOverSea
reviews.text: Today I decided to visit Chùa Dơi (Bat Pagoda) in Sóc Trăng, and it turned out to be a truly fascinating stop. The old wooden temple immediately stood out as it is clearly influenced by Khmer Theravāda Buddhist architecture, with its ornate details and the beautiful, colorful paintings inside, and it still carries that unmistakable Khmer spirit, which makes the atmosphere feel both sacred and culturally rich. I learned that the temple, also known as Mahatup Pagoda, was originally built in the 16th century by the Khmer community and has been an important spiritual and cultural center for more than 400 years. It’s also famous for the thousands of fruit bats that live peacefully in the tall trees around the grounds, which is indeed unique feature that gives the place its nickname, “The Bat Pagoda”. Although the backyard area of the temple feels a bit untidy, the overall experience is still very worthwhile. The history, the architecture, and the serene atmosphere make it a meaningful place to visit if you’re exploring Sóc Trăng. Highly recommended for anyone interested in Khmer culture, Buddhist heritage, or unique spiritual sites in the Mekong Delta.

reviews.text: It's famous not because of nice architecture temple but a lot of really big bats (Size of eagle) in the garden. They only eat fruit but the size no other bat can reach.

reviews.author: Alan Le
reviews.text: Iconic spiritual place of Soc Trang province. Must pay a visit here when you have a chance traveling here. There re lots of bats living high on the trees in the garden of this temple, very big bats, and the place is very noisy with those sound from these animals

reviews.author: FrancisP
reviews.text: Beautiful & long history Khmer pagoda. This place is famous with many bats living here

reviews.author: Nguyễn Tuấn Hiền
reviews.text: I paid a quick visit to Mahaup pagoda due to its famous architecture and its bats (mostly its bats). It was smaller than I thought. One biggest building and few smaller towers, some were in reconstruction. I am not an intellectually architectural man but honestly speaking, the architecture of this pagoda is somehow beautiful to me. The bats were still crowded but not as crowded as they used to be (according to local taxi driver who drove me there). But they were giantic compared to these tiny bats I encountered in the big city. They were hanging in groups in the backside of pagoda. I were surprised of how they live peacefully right on top of human made buildings and many other human activities. I give 4 stars for the architecture and the bats. 1 star is deducted due to bad hygiene (trash is mostly everywhere in the backside of pagoda, maybe due to the reconstruction). P/S: it is free. No ticket needed.

reviews.author: ASEANplus3travels
reviews.text: The pagoda is ok, but the main attraction is the resident fruitbats, which leave the canopy of the trees at dusk. They are mostly in the trees in the right backquarter of the compound. We had a good view from the path behind the central hall and the monastery.

reviews.author: Chris
reviews.text: A Cambodian temple full of protected bats. These are sacred creatures and live on trees just next to the temple. Finches could be bought and released as an offering for Buddha's blessing.

reviews.text: It was super nice to see some big bats which are in the trees around the temple (you have to look all around because they are only in 3 or 4 trees). I also enjoyed in the evening all the food that was proposed inside the area of the the temple. I removed 2 stars because of the super heavy music which is on, it is super annoying and not good for the bats.

reviews.author: Heinrich “Hein” Lombard
reviews.text: My favorite Buddhist pagoda in Soc Trang. Brings back fond memories of rural Cambodia every time I go there. Monks are friendly and welcoming. The sound of traditional Khmer music and the smell of burning incense captivates the imagination.

reviews.author: 최현우 (Choi Hyunwoo)
reviews.text: Chùa Dơi (Bats' Pagoda) in Soc Trang, Vietnam, is a unique and fascinating religious site with a rich history. Here's a review in English: Chùa Dơi, or Bats' Pagoda, is an enchanting place of worship nestled in the heart of Soc Trang province in Vietnam. This pagoda is renowned for its distinctive architectural style and the thousands of fruit bats that call it home, making it a truly unique and captivating destination. Architectural Marvel: Chùa Dơi's architecture is a blend of Khmer and Chinese influences, characterized by intricate carvings, colorful details, and towering pagodas. The pagoda's design is a testament to the region's cultural diversity and religious harmony. Breathtaking Scenery: The pagoda's serene surroundings, including lush gardens and lotus ponds, create a tranquil atmosphere that is perfect for meditation and reflection. The sight of bats hanging from the trees and pagoda structures adds to the mystique of the place. Spiritual Experience: Chùa Dơi is a significant religious site for Buddhists and visitors seeking spiritual enlightenment. Inside the pagoda, you can witness beautiful altars, sculptures, and religious artifacts that showcase the rich Buddhist traditions. Unique Bat Colony: The pagoda is most famous for its bat colony, which resides in the trees and pagoda roofs. At dusk, thousands of bats emerge in search of food, creating a mesmerizing spectacle. It's a sight that you won't find at most other pagodas. Cultural Exploration: Exploring Chùa Dơi offers a glimpse into the diverse cultural fabric of Soc Trang. Visitors can witness local religious rituals, interact with friendly monks, and learn about the history of Buddhism in the region. Photographer's Paradise: The vibrant colors, intricate details, and the enchanting presence of bats make Chùa Dơi a paradise for photographers and art enthusiasts. Every corner of the pagoda offers a unique photo opportunity. Local Hospitality: The local community is welcoming and often engages with visitors, sharing stories and insights into the pagoda's significance. Chùa Dơi is not only a place of religious importance but also a cultural and natural wonder. Its unique blend of spirituality and the natural world makes it a must-visit destination for travelers exploring the Mekong Delta region in Vietnam. Whether you are seeking spiritual solace or simply appreciating its beauty, Chùa Dơi leaves an indelible impression on all who visit.

Dung dau vo may cho goi xe doi dien chua. 7k/chiec Lua dao ngay tai chua:)) Drive your bike in the temple yard, tou can park there for free. Do not use any parking lots across the temple. They charged 7k/bike upfront cuz they know what they're doing:))

reviews.author: Nguyen Duc Nguyen
reviews.text: All bats are gone. Not much to see. Just average. A lot of trash too.

reviews.author: STRUONG Cosmetic
reviews.text: Oder reaps through the air onced passed through the entrance. This place also know as bats temple. The interesting thing is that the temple was burned down several times when it was first built. Not because of some or spirit did it but it was caused by something more interesting. During cold nights, bats care for their young by finding something warm for their young. Warm coal readily available in the temple kitchen. These coals even though not burning on the surface but within their core is still hot. With slight breeze can cause a spark that lit up the bats nest. As one can imagine with dried leaves and twigs leads to while fire.

reviews.author: Mr. RaTha
reviews.text: Chua Doi know as a family name is Bat pagoda is located in Soc Trang province, Vietnam. It is attracted many tourists in and out country come and visit it. The best time in day you can see bats round 8 am to 4 pm after that time bats will fly out of the campus of pagoda.
